points.text: Intellia Therapeutics reported positive Phase 3 results for lonvo-z, an in vivo CRISPR gene editing therapy for hereditary angioedema, achieving an 87% reduction in swelling attacks versus placebo.
Intellia Therapeutics initiated a rolling BLA submission to the FDA for lonvo-z following the HAELO trial success, targeting a U.S. launch in the first half of 2027.

The HAELO trial enrolled 80 patients (52 treated, 28 placebo) and demonstrated that 62% of lonvo-z recipients achieved freedom from attacks or need for other therapies compared to 11% of placebo patients.

ElevateBio presented nine abstracts at the ASGCT 29th Annual Meeting showcasing advancements in CRISPR systems, including protein and gRNA engineering to optimize adenosine base editor activity for monogenic disease correction.

Editas Medicine secured a third favorable U.S. Patent and Trademark Office decision affirming Broad Institute's priority for CRISPR/Cas9 in eukaryotic cells, reinforcing IP for EDIT-401 hyperlipidemia therapy.
Editas Medicine reported EDIT-401, a CRISPR/Cas9-based therapy, achieved greater than 90% mean LDL cholesterol reduction in non-human primates in preclinical studies.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals and CRISPR Therapeutics' exagamglogene autotemcel (Casgevy) utilizes CRISPR-Cas9 to edit the BCL11A gene, inducing fetal hemoglobin production for sickle cell disease treatment.
A modified CRISPR tool is being developed to target the Down syndrome mutation, representing a new application of the technology beyond monogenic blood disorders.

Eli Lilly entered a collaboration with AI-focused biotech Profluent to develop next-generation gene editors capable of inserting entire genes, with potential milestone payments totaling $2.25 billion.
Intellia Therapeutics announced a proposed underwritten public offering of $150 million in common stock to fund CRISPR gene editing programs including lonvoguran ziclumeran.
Intellia Therapeutics — lonvo-z — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 HAELO trial met primary endpoint with 87% reduction in attack rate vs placebo; rolling BLA submitted to FDA; launch planned H1 2027.

ElevateBio — CRISPR-based adenosine base editors — Monogenic diseases — Presented poster #1303 detailing protein and gRNA engineering strategies to optimize adenosine base editor activity for causative mutation correction.

Editas Medicine — CRISPR/Cas9 Patent Interference — Intellectual Property — U.S. PTAB reaffirmed Broad Institute's inventorship priority for CRISPR/Cas9 in human cells, marking third favorable decision against CVC.

Vertex Pharmaceuticals / CRISPR Therapeutics — exagamglogene autotemcel (Casgevy) — Sickle Cell Disease — FDA-approved CRISPR-Cas9 therapy targeting BCL11A gene to restore fetal hemoglobin; addresses underlying cause in patients aged 12+ with recurrent vaso-occlusive crises.
Unspecified Entity — Modified CRISPR tool — Down syndrome — Preclinical/early development stage targeting specific chromosomal mutation associated with Down syndrome.

Eli Lilly — Profluent collaboration — Gene editing (CRISPR-associated) — Deal includes potential $2.25B in milestone payments to develop new gene editors for gene insertion; specific programs, diseases, and upfront fees undisclosed.
Intellia Therapeutics — Commercialization of first in vivo CRISPR medicine — lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Phase 3 positive; rolling BLA filed; preparing for H1 2027 U.S. launch.

ElevateBio — Expansion of gene editing toolbox beyond nuclease editing — CRISPR systems + base editing + epigenetic editing — Presenting data on large gene insertion, AI-driven protein discovery, and LNP delivery at ASGCT 2026.
Editas Medicine — CRISPR/Cas9 commercialization — EDIT-401 (hyperlipidemia) — Secured foundational IP rights via PTAB victory; preclinical data shows >90% LDL reduction in NHPs.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Commercialization of gene editing therapies — exagamglogene autotemcel (Casgevy) — Partnered with Vertex Pharmaceuticals; CRISPR-Cas9 mechanism approved for sickle cell disease; access challenges noted due to high costs and manufacturing complexity.

Eli Lilly — Expansion into genetic medicine — CRISPR/gene editing pipeline via Profluent partnership — Leveraging obesity drug revenues to fund new Boston genetic medicine center and acquire gene therapy assets.

Profluent — AI-driven gene editor development — Next-generation CRISPR tools — Partnered with Eli Lilly to create gene editors capable of full gene insertion; financial terms include up to $2.25B in milestones.

lonvo-z — Mechanism: In vivo CRISPR gene editing to inactivate KLKB1 gene and lower bradykinin — Phase: 3 (HAELO) — Population: Adults/adolescents (≥16) with Type I/II HAE — Comparator: Placebo — Endpoints: 87% attack rate reduction; 62% attack-free vs 11% placebo; 50 mg one-time dose.

Adenosine base editors — Mechanism: CRISPR-guided protein and gRNA engineering — Phase: Preclinical discovery — Population: Monogenic disease models — Endpoints: Optimization of editing activity for causative mutation correction via poster #1303.
EDIT-401 — Mechanism: CRISPR/Cas9 in vivo gene editing — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Non-human primates — Comparator: None specified — Results: >90% mean LDL cholesterol reduction achieved.

exagamglogene autotemcel — Mechanism: CRISPR-Cas9 editing of BCL11A gene to upregulate fetal hemoglobin — Phase: Approved (Post-market) — Population: Sickle cell disease patients aged 12+ with recurrent vaso-occlusive crises — Comparator: None (curative intent) — Results: Normalization of total hemoglobin levels and durable fetal hemoglobin expression reported in NEJM study.

Profluent/Eli Lilly joint program — Mechanism: AI-designed gene editors for whole-gene insertion — Phase: Preclinical/early development — Population: Not specified — Comparator: N/A — Status: Collaboration announced; specific indications and trial timelines not disclosed.
Intellia Therapeutics — Regulatory filing and commercial preparation — lonvo-z — Rolling BLA submitted to FDA; potential U.S. launch scheduled for first half of 2027.
Intellia Therapeutics — Proposed public offering of common stock — Corporate financing — Announced April 27, 2026 to support operations and CRISPR pipeline advancement.
Intellia Therapeutics — Capital raise — Common stock offering — Proposed $150M underwritten public offering with 15% over-allotment option to fund CRISPR pipeline development.

Intellia Therapeutics — Capital raise — Common Stock — Launched $150 million underwritten public offering with 15% over-allotment option; Jefferies, Goldman Sachs, and Citigroup acting as joint book-runners.

Eli Lilly / Profluent — Strategic collaboration for gene editing — CRISPR-related technology — Potential $2.25B in milestone payments; upfront payment amount undisclosed; part of Lilly's broader gene therapy acquisition strategy.
